steps.title: Spot the opening contrast
steps.content: The passage opens with an explicit contrast—”The sermon is [a sound]... a book printed is a record.” This immediately signals that Defoe is comparing two things and will detail what distinguishes them.

steps.title: Match to the correct purpose
steps.content: Choice D says the passage “describes differences in the characteristics of two kinds of communication.” It does exactly that—sermons are ephemeral and memory-dependent, while books are permanent records. A, B, and C reference elements not present in the passage: critical reception, scholars' contributions, and usage frequency. This matches choice D — D is correct.
